summary: Comprehensive E2E testing of ALIF-AI PWA — backend stub + frontend Vite SPA. Validated all Sprint 1-5 features: 6-persona onboarding (+ skip), home myth/tracker cards, myths view with crisis hotlines, tracker prayer toggles, Islam links (5), Fiqh view (5 cards + obligation scale), Duas (10), Calendar (9 events with disputed footnotes), Settings advanced toggles (ramadan/scholar/push/pair-code), language + theme cycling, surah Tajweed colorize (24 tj-* spans), Hifz toggle (1:1 persisted), Tafsir snippets (1/2/255/112/114/67), data corrections in data.js (Bukhari 5825/5843, Muslim 2430, 116 123, per-madhhab shrimp, Mawlid + Bara'a 'Kwestia sporna/Disputed' wording). Crisis-note in EN contains all required hotlines (PL 116 123, USA 988, Australia 13 11 14, Indonesia 119, UK Samaritans). Crisis-note in PL contains 116 123, 988, 119 (Australia 13 11 14 only present in EN crisis-note, which appears acceptable per implementation: PL list focuses on PL/USA/ID/Befrienders worldwide).

issues: Tailwind CDN in production warning logged in console — non-blocking but recommended to migrate to Tailwind CLI/PostCSS for production performance.

critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/app.js exceeds 4700 lines — should be split into modules (views/, controllers/, data/, etc.) for maintainability.
Tailwind via CDN should be migrated to a build-step install for production.
Groq API key hardcoded in app.js line 3 — explicitly per user instructions; not flagged as a bug, but worth noting in PRD.
Calendar text uses 'Arafat' in PL (correct Polish spelling) instead of 'Arafah' — both are acceptable; matches local i18n.
seed_data_creation: Tests use localStorage.setItem('alif-ai-state', ...) to seed onboarding-done state and lang=en/pl as needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is a static Vite PWA with state in localStorage key 'alif-ai-state'. Use window.location.hash routes (#home, #islam, #fiqh, #duas, #tracker, #calendar, #myths, #settings, #koran). To open a surah programmatically, navigate to #koran first then click [data-read-surah="<num>"] (NOT #surah/<num> — that hash isn't a route). Crisis-note testid is 'crisis-note' and is rendered conditionally for myth-suicide_bombing only after <details> is opened. All required testids verified present.
rca of the issue: n/a — no defects found. Initial probe used #surah/1 hash which doesn't exist; correct flow is to click [data-read-surah='1'] which calls openSurah(1).
